citations.narrative:
During the review of records, the licensee was unable to provide complete acquisition and disposition records for some
animals acquired by the facility:
***There are no acquisition records present for two adult male degus and three adult black-tailed prairie dogs.
***There are incomplete acquisition records for three adult pigeons, one adult male chinchilla and one adult female
chinchilla.
Failure to maintain complete acquisition and disposition records interferes with the ability to accurately identify animals,
trace their movements and identify trends in animal losses. Every dealer and exhibitor shall make, keep and maintain
records or forms that fully and correctly disclose the required information, which includes: The name and address of the
person from whom the animals were acquired, the USDA license number of the person if licensed or the vehicle license
number and state and driver’s license number and state if not licensed, the name and address of the person to whom an
animal was given/sold, the date of acquisition or disposition, the species of animal and the number of animals involved.
The records shall include any offspring born of any animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control.
TO BE CORRECTED FROM THE DATE OF THIS INSPECTION ON 05/14/2026 AND GOING FORWARD

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The enclosures housing three, adult pigeons have an accumulation of urates and debris on the floors and other surfaces.
The facility representative told the inspector that these enclosures are cleaned once per week.
Accumulations of excreta and other material are porous and can harbor microorganisms that could cause animal illness.
The licensee must ensure that primary enclosures for birds are sanitized as often as necessary to prevent such
accumulations and other disease hazards.
TO BE CORRECTED BY 05/18/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
During the review of records, the licensee was unable to provide complete acquisition and disposition records for some 
animals acquired by the facility: 
***There are no acquisition records present for two adult male degus and three adult black-tailed prairie dogs. 
***There are incomplete acquisition records for three adult pigeons, one adult male chinchilla and one adult female 
chinchilla. 
Failure to maintain complete acquisition and disposition records interferes with the ability to accurately identify animals, 
trace their movements and identify trends in animal losses. Every dealer and exhibitor shall make, keep and maintain 
records or forms that fully and correctly disclose the required information, which includes: The name and address of the 
person from whom the animals were acquired, the USDA license number of the person if licensed or the vehicle license 
number and state and driver’s license number and state if not licensed, the name and address of the person to whom an 
animal was given/sold, the date of acquisition or disposition, the species of animal and the number of animals involved. 
The records shall include any offspring born of any animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control. 
TO BE CORRECTED FROM THE DATE OF THIS INSPECTION ON 05/14/2026 AND GOING FORWARD 
3.158(a)(1) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The enclosures housing three, adult pigeons have an accumulation of urates and debris on the floors and other surfaces. 
The facility representative told the inspector that these enclosures are cleaned once per week. 
Accumulations of excreta and other material are porous and can harbor microorganisms that could cause animal illness. 
The licensee must ensure that primary enclosures for birds are sanitized as often as necessary to prevent such 
accumulations and other disease hazards. 
TO BE CORRECTED BY 05/18/2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
